title: Spiced Veg Sandwich

subs.name: paneer slices
subs.notes: makes it richer and higher protein

subs.name: only coriander leaves if mint is unavailable
subs.notes: chutney is greener and earthier — still works

steps.text: Blend mint, coriander leaves, green chilli, lemon juice, cumin, a pinch of salt, and 1 tablespoon of water into a smooth green chutney. Taste and adjust tartness and heat.

Spread the green chutney generously on one side of each bread slice. Arrange the boiled potato slices, tomato, cucumber, capsicum, and onion on two of the slices. Season the filling with a pinch of salt and cumin powder.
Press the remaining chutney-spread bread slices firmly on top to form two sandwiches. Press down with your palm to compact the filling.
Heat a tawa on medium. Brush the outsides with butter or oil and toast the sandwiches 2 minutes per side, pressing with a flat spatula, until the bread is golden and crisp.
Cut diagonally and serve immediately with tomato ketchup on the side. The sandwich loses its crunch quickly, so eat it fresh.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mint → only coriander leaves if mint is unavailable (chutney is greener and earthier — still works); potato → paneer slices (makes it richer and higher protein); bread → whole wheat or multigrain bread (healthier option — same method)
